sale.introDesc: iHui Open Platform is distributed as semi-open source on GitHub: core frontend and open capabilities can be viewed, learned, and used within the agreed scope; some backend and commercial modules remain proprietary. Suitable for enterprise customization, on-premises deployment, and compliance audits.
sale.whyChoose1: Transparent pricing: Free tier, Standard ¥9,999/year, Enterprise on request—no hidden fees
sale.whyChoose2: Source code auditable: Core frontend and open API source viewable and modifiable for compliance and customization
sale.whyChoose3: Multi-platform: PC site plus WeChat/Alipay miniprograms, H5, APP, Electron—one codebase, multiple deployments
sale.whyChoose4: Dual backend: Java for business, Python for AI; independently scalable and on-prem deployable
sale.whyChoose5: Enterprise support: Community/ticket support, SLA and dedicated CSM available
sale.whyChoose6: Self-developed IHUI AI architecture: orchestration and multimodal pipeline under your control; on-prem and customizable
sale.whyChoose7: iHui API (IHUI API) unified open API: protocol-compatible, one-stop access, low latency and high availability
sale.pricingSubtitle: Transparent pricing; each tier includes a clear feature set.
sale.planFreeDesc: Try platform capabilities at no cost; credits on signup.
sale.planStandardDesc: Source + docs + community support, for teams and SMBs.
sale.planEnterpriseDesc: On-prem, SLA, and dedicated support; quote on request.
sale.contactPrice: For exact pricing and discounts, see the GitHub repo or contact sales.
sale.rule1: Channels: Sold via GitHub Releases or via website/contract.
sale.rule2: Payment & invoicing: Bank transfer and online payment; VAT invoices available.
sale.rule3: License scope: Purchasers get source access and usage rights for the version; redistribution and commercial resale are prohibited.
sale.rule4: Refunds: Digital goods are generally non-refundable after delivery; exceptions at discretion of support.
sale.rule5: Renewal & upgrades: Subscriptions renew yearly; perpetual licenses get updates for an agreed period; major upgrades may be separate.
sale.licenseName: iHui Open Platform Commercial Source License (Semi-Open Source License)
sale.licenseSummary: You may use, modify, and deploy the code internally under these terms: viewing, learning, modifying, and use in your own business are allowed; redistribution, resale, or use in products that compete directly with iHui AI is prohibited. Full terms are in the LICENSE file provided with purchase.
sale.delivery1: GitHub private repo access or Release source archive.
sale.delivery2: Full docs, API reference, and integration guides.
sale.delivery3: Bug fixes and security updates for the agreed period (per version).
sale.delivery4: Standard includes community/ticket support; Enterprise includes dedicated support and SLA.
sale.readmeSummary: The repo includes a README with quick start, requirements, build commands, and structure; after purchase you also get deployment and configuration guides.
sale.introDesc2: Semi-open source balances transparency with commercial protection: you get full source for the frontend and open APIs for customization, on-prem deployment, and security audits; we retain proprietary rights to selected backend and commercial components to fund ongoing R&D and support.
sale.introDesc3: Whether you are a small team integrating LLM and multimodal capabilities or an enterprise needing private deployment and dedicated support, you can choose the right tier from the pricing and sales policy below.
sale.whoIsItFor1: Enterprise dev teams integrating AI into their systems and building on top of the platform
sale.whoIsItFor2: Finance, healthcare, government, and other sectors requiring source-level audits and compliance
sale.whoIsItFor3: Organizations that need on-prem or private-cloud deployment with data residency
sale.whoIsItFor4: Developers integrating deeply with n8n, Dify, and other workflow platforms
sale.useCase1: Enterprise AI hub: unified access to multiple models and modalities, exposing standardized APIs internally and externally
sale.useCase2: Vertical solution providers: customizing and shipping AI products for specific industries
sale.useCase3: DevOps and security: hardening, performance tuning, and internal training using the source code
sale.useCase4: Procurement and legal: evaluating vendors and IP compliance with access to source and documentation
sale.useCase5: Education and research: using the platform for teaching, experiments, and reproducibility within the license terms
sale.casesSubtitle: Typical use cases and outcomes (replace with real customer stories as needed)
sale.case1Desc: Swarm and document APIs for second-level report parsing and risk identification; supports credit review and compliance audits.
sale.case2Desc: Image/text and voice capabilities from product modeling to pre- and post-sales support; lower cost, higher conversion.
sale.case3Desc: Full-format document API for contracts and official docs: clause comparison, compliance checks, and audit trails.
sale.comparisonTitle: iHui vs self-build, SaaS, and open source
sale.comparisonDesc: iHui Open Platform sits between full self-build, pure SaaS, and full open source: you get deployable source and transparent pricing, plus ongoing updates and optional enterprise support.
sale.compareVsSelf1: Save months of R&D and ops; get multi-platform and AI capability matrix out of the box
sale.compareVsSelf2: Source is editable and auditable; you can still customize and deploy on-prem
sale.compareVsSaaS1: Data and traffic stay in your environment; meets data residency and compliance needs
sale.compareVsSaaS2: One-time or annual fee for source and license; no long-term usage lock-in
sale.compareVsOpen1: Commercial license restricts redistribution and resale; we provide focused support and updates
sale.compareVsOpen2: Semi-open: core frontend and open API are viewable and modifiable; some backend by version
sale.saleFaq1q: What is the main difference between Standard and Enterprise?
sale.saleFaq1a: Standard includes source access, full documentation, and community/ticket support—suited for teams and SMBs. Enterprise adds on-prem deployment support, SLA, and a dedicated technical account manager—suited for larger organizations with higher availability and compliance requirements.
sale.saleFaq2q: How do I get the source code and updates after purchase?
sale.saleFaq2a: After purchase you receive access to a private GitHub repo or a Release source archive for the agreed version. During the subscription or perpetual term you get bug fixes and security updates via the repo or our channels; major version upgrades may be priced separately.
sale.saleFaq3q: Is there a discount for education or non-profits?
sale.saleFaq3a: Yes. Educational and non-profit organizations can request a discount or special license with valid proof; terms are subject to sales confirmation.
sale.saleFaq4q: Does the license allow integration and delivery in customer projects?
sale.saleFaq4a: Yes, you may integrate, modify, and deploy the platform in your own products and in projects you deliver to end customers. You may not redistribute or resell the platform source or derivatives as a standalone product, or use it in products that compete directly with iHui AI. See the LICENSE for full terms.

agentScenario.heroSubtitle: Based on task execution complexity and autonomous decision-making dependence, helping Enterprises identify and plan agent application scenarios to find the most suitable AI implementation path.

overview.title: Understanding the Agent Scenario Compass
overview.description: The Agent Scenario Compass is an analytical framework to help Enterprises identify and plan AI agent application scenarios. Through two dimensions - task execution complexity and autonomous decision-making dependence - agent applications are divided into four quadrants to help Enterprises choose the most suitable implementation path.
overview.keyPointText: Different quadrants have varying requirements for technical capabilities, deployment costs, and risk management. Enterprises should start from the bottom-left quadrant (simple decision + simple execution) and gradually expand to other quadrants after accumulating experience.

simple.title: Efficient Assistant - Simple Decision & Simple Execution Agent
simple.fullDescription: These agents are in the bottom-left quadrant of the compass, characterized by low autonomous decision dependence and low task execution complexity. User intent is clear, and the agent only needs to complete relatively simple tasks, typically involving few steps and API calls.

workflow.name: Workflow Automation Assistant
workflow.desc: Automate repetitive workflows like data entry and report generation

simple.value: Lowest technical barrier, fastest deployment, suitable for the first step of Enterprise AI. Can quickly generate value and build team confidence in AI.

decision.title: Wise Advisor - Complex Decision Agent
decision.fullDescription: These agents are in the top-left quadrant, characterized by high autonomous decision dependence but low task execution complexity. Users ask open-ended questions requiring deep thinking and analysis, but final execution is relatively simple (like generating reports or suggestions).

analysis.name: Data Analysis Advisor
analysis.desc: Deep analysis of business data, discovering insights and providing visualization reports

advisor.name: Decision Support Advisor
advisor.desc: Integrating multi-dimensional information to provide decision suggestions for management

decision.value: Core value lies in reducing decision costs and improving decision quality. Suitable for scenarios requiring deep analysis but simple execution.

execution.title: Execution Expert - Complex Execution Agent
execution.fullDescription: These agents are in the bottom-right quadrant, characterized by low autonomous decision dependence but high task execution complexity. User intent is very clear, but completing the task requires coordinating multiple systems, calling multiple APIs, and executing complex processes.

meeting.name: Meeting Scheduling Assistant
meeting.desc: Coordinating multi-party schedules, booking meeting rooms, sending invitations, preparing materials

approval.name: Approval Process Assistant
approval.desc: Automating complex approval processes, cross-department coordination, document circulation

execution.value: Core value lies in liberating human resources and handling complex cross-system tasks. Requires good system integration capabilities.

complex.title: Omnipotent Expert - Complex Decision & Complex Execution Agent
complex.fullDescription: These agents are in the top-right quadrant, the most complex type. They need to handle open-ended tasks with high uncertainty, autonomously plan and make decisions, while the execution process also involves multi-system collaboration and long-cycle management.

marketing.name: Marketing Campaign Automation
marketing.desc: Full-process automation from market research to campaign planning, execution, and performance tracking

multiAgent.name: Multi-Agent Collaboration System
multiAgent.desc: Multiple specialized agents working together to complete complex projects

complex.value: Represents the highest level of AI application, requiring powerful large model capabilities and comprehensive infrastructure. Recommended to attempt after accumulating sufficient experience.

guide.title: Enterprise Implementation Suggestions
guide.intro: Based on the Agent Scenario Compass, we recommend Enterprises adopt a progressive AI implementation strategy, starting from simple scenarios and gradually accumulating experience and capabilities.
